first_paragraph: ***The Good Life*** ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *La buena vida*) is a 2008 [drama film](/wiki/Drama_(film_and_television) "Drama (film and television)") directed by [Andrés Wood](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9s_Wood "Andrés Wood") and written by [Mamoun Hassan](/wiki/Mamoun_Hassan "Mamoun Hassan"). The film won the 2008 [Goya Award for Best Spanish Language Foreign Film](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Spanish_Language_Foreign_Film "Goya Award for Best Spanish Language Foreign Film").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

***The Good Life*** ([Spanish](/wiki/Spanish_language "Spanish language"): *La buena vida*) is a 2008 [drama film](/wiki/Drama_(film_and_television) "Drama (film and television)") directed by [Andrés Wood](/wiki/Andr%C3%A9s_Wood "Andrés Wood") and written by [Mamoun Hassan](/wiki/Mamoun_Hassan "Mamoun Hassan"). The film won the 2008 [Goya Award for Best Spanish Language Foreign Film](/wiki/Goya_Award_for_Best_Spanish_Language_Foreign_Film "Goya Award for Best Spanish Language Foreign Film").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Plot

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Good_Life_(2008_film)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Plot")]

Teresa ([Aline Küppenheim](/wiki/Aline_K%C3%BCppenheim "Aline Küppenheim")), Edmundo ([Roberto Farias](/wiki/Roberto_Far%C3%ADas "Roberto Farías")), Mario (Eduardo Paxeco), and Patricia (Paula Sotelo) are four inhabitants of the city of [Santiago de Chile](/wiki/Santiago "Santiago") whose lives intersect in public places but rarely come together to communicate.

Immersed in the urban whirlwind, each of them pursues a goal: Teresa, a social psychologist who helps women in situations of risk; Edmundo, a stylist who still lives with his mother and longs to have a car; Mario, a young [clarinetist](/wiki/Clarinet "Clarinet") who arrives from [Berlin](/wiki/Berlin "Berlin") and wants to join the Philharmonic; and Patricia, who struggles to survive while caring for a baby and battling illness.

Four stories based on real events, very different from each other but united by the ups and downs and contradictions of life in a South American city.

* Paula Sotelo - Patricia

* [Manuela Martelli](/wiki/Manuela_Martelli "Manuela Martelli") - Paula

* [Bélgica Castro](/wiki/B%C3%A9lgica_Castro "Bélgica Castro") - Leonor
